items.next_action: Reframe H5 for phone-legible review after the first three-panel comparison failed owner discrimination at phone scale: use one large subject or stage per image, preserve the mature semi-realistic restrained toon/cel direction, and retain the selective-aging clarification as a later engine-neutral lifecycle proof obligation rather than visual or runtime implementation authority.
items.proof: R0.2-R0.5 establish compiler/bootstrap continuity. H1 proves reference provenance and the absence of a recovered mesh or rig. H2 proves a strict 17-joint structural profile; H3 proves deterministic capability-free generation with fixed candidate fingerprint 4d04df0dd58cdd8ecdb7c41e9dbde2dec1910b36b7d5643b2d254ef4b3c707fa. H4 binds that candidate and the exact reference/control fingerprints into an orthogonal integer matrix: edge deficit 1 detects only broken connection, rest front-span loss 480 detects only silhouette collapse, and frame-one hand vertical displacement 480 detects only articulation drift. Five H4 tests reject missing controls, stale bindings, cross-sensitive or post-hoc claims, unknown fields, and noncanonical bytes; 18-module boundaries and the complete Forge gate pass. P13-P15 enforce pixel-level asset fitness, substage-bound macro/micro refresh, and cheapest-sufficient simulation. Original-resolution inspection of Forge capture c85653f3329d164ea07f0dca334de05879faab4d17e65ac00ab1e9eed764840e confirms a clean UI capture but only a tiny stick skeleton, so it is rejected as a good-quality human reference while retained for structural tests. Nothing approves, promotes, proves anatomy or preference, selects an engine, or mutates the protected Kernel.
